title: Super Star Wars
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Redlink}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:Superstarwars.jpg]] |title=''Super Star Wars'' |developer=*[[LucasArts]] *[[Sculptured Software]] |publisher=[[JVC]] |release date=[[November 30]], [[1992]] {{C|[[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|SNES]]}}{{WebCite|url=https://publicrecords.copyright.gov/detailed-record/voyager_14923151|text=Super Star Wars.|work=the [[Wikipedia:United States Copyright Office|U.S. Copyright Office Public Records System]]|archivedate=20250724104548}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=[[Wikipedia:Action game|Action game]] |modes=[[Wikipedia:Single-player|Single player]] |ratings=[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]: Everyone (E), [[Wikipedia:Entertainment and Leisure Software Publishers Association|ELSPA]]: 3+ |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|Super Nintendo Entertainment System]], [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]] (Virtual Console), [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 4|PlayStation 4]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation Vita|PlayStation Vita]] |series= |basegame= |expansions= |director=[[Kalani Streicher]]{{WebCite|url=https://archive.org/details/super-star-wars-usa-rev-1|text=''Super Star Wars Instruction Booklet''|work=The Internet Archive|nobackup=1}} |producer= |designer=Kalani Streicher |programmer=[[Peter Ward]] |artist=[[Harrison Fong]] (Art Supervision) |writer= |composer=[[Paul Webb]] (Based on [[John Williams]]) |timeline= |prev= |next=[[Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] }} '''''Super Star Wars''''' is the first of a series of three [[Wikipedia:Super Nintendo Entertainment System|Super Nintendo]] games based on the original three films of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' series, released [[November 30]], [[1992]]. The plot is based on {{Film|IV}}. In [[2009]] the game was released on the [[Wikipedia:Wii|Wii]]'s Virtual Console and on November 17, [[2015]], it was ported to [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 4|PlayStation 4]] and Vita. The term ''Super Star Wars'' can refer to the first game or the entire series. All are essentially platform games, although they all have stages which feature other challenges, such as driving a [[landspeeder]] or piloting an [[X-wing starfighter|X-wing fighter]]. All three also feature multiple playable characters with different abilities. The game's manual added a number of names and details not spelled out in the game itself. The ''[[Super Star Wars Official Game Secrets]]'' guide released separately added even more details.''[[Super Star Wars Official Game Secrets]]'' ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Episode IV
A NEW HOPE |text=It is a period of civil war. Rebel spaceships, striking from a hidden base, have won their first victory against the evil Galactic Empire. During the battle, Rebel spies managed to steal secret plans to the Empire's ultimate weapon, the DEATH STAR, an armored space station with enough power to destroy an entire planet. Pursued by the Empire's sinister agents, Princess Leia races home aboard her starship, custodian of the stolen plans that can save her people and restore freedom to the galaxy....}} ==Gameplay== ''Super Star Wars'' generally follows the [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] plot closely, with some changes made to suit a platform action game. Characters have to fight their way from scene to scene. Instead of simply buying the [[droid]]s from the [[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] meets [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]] in the desert, then fights his way into and through the Jawa [[Sandcrawler/Legends|Sandcrawler]] to rescue [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. The player characters, not [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], shut down the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] after fighting a number of enemies. The player is able to initially control Luke Skywalker, then can choose [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] or [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] as the game progresses. Other levels have players control Luke's [[X-34 landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] and an X-wing fighter. At one point, the game was to include a [[Garbage Compactor 3263827|garbage compactor]] level, where the [[Dianoga/Legends|Dianoga]] would likely be the boss. Early press images included a screenshot from that scene.{{BookCite|container=''Super Star Wars'' - EGM Preview!|title=Electronic Gaming Monthly|number=No. 37|link=https://archive.org/details/Electronic_Gaming_Monthly_37/mode/2up}} ===Levels=== #'''[[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]]''' - Boss: [[Sarlacc/Legends|Sarlacc Pit Monster]] #'''Tatooine I''' (Landspeeder, Level 1) #'''Outside the [[Digger crawler|Sandcrawler]]''' #'''Inside the Sandcrawler''' - Boss: [[Jawenko lava beast|Lava Beast Jawenko]] #'''Part I: Land of the [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Sandpeople]]''' / '''Part II: Land of the [[Bantha/Legends|Banthas]]''' - Boss: [[Mutant Womprat]] #'''Tatooine II''' (Landspeeder to [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]]) #'''Mos Eisley''' #'''[[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Cantina]] Fight''' - Boss: [[Kalhar/Legends|Kalhar Boss Monster]] #'''Escape from Mos Eisley''' - Boss: [[Hover Combat Carrier]] #'''[[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] [[Docking Bay 327/Legends|Bay]]''' - Boss: [[B1-series worker droid|Imperial Defense Droid]] #'''Rescue of the [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Princess]]''' - Boss: [[Detention Guard repulsorcraft|Detention Guard Boss]] #'''[[Tractor beam/Legends|Tractor Beam]] Core''' #'''[[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Death Star Attack]]''' #'''[[Meridian trench/Legends|Trench]] [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle]]''' - Boss: [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|TIE Advanced]] ===Weapons=== *[[Blaster/Legends|Laser Blaster]] *[[Flame blaster]] *[[Proton Seeker]] *[[Rapid ion gun]] *[[Plasma Wave Blaster]] *[[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsaber]] {{C|only as Luke Skywalker}} ===Power-Ups=== *[[Plasma shield/Legends|Plasma shield]] - Temporary invulnerability *[[Thermal detonator/Legends|Thermal detonator]] - Clears screen of enemies *Health (Small and Large) - Regains health *Time - Increases time to finish the level *Bonus - Multiplies the number of points by 2 *Health Sword - Increases size of health bar *Extra Life - One more life ===Enemies=== The game's manual refers to many Cantina aliens by their individual names, but since multiple versions appear fighting in one scene this list assumes there are also other, multiple members of those species present.
*[[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] (including [[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba]]) *[[Bantha/Legends|Bantha]] (mini-boss) *[[Bounty hunter/Legends|Bounty hunters]] *[[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronians]] (including [[Kardue'sai'Malloc/Legends|Labria]]) *[[Duros/Legends|Duros]] *[[Earth-rock|Boulders]] *[[Eyeball Droid]]s *[[Factory droid]]s *[[GNK power droid/Legends|GNK power droids]] *[[Imperial spider droid]]s *[[IT-O interrogation droid/Legends|IT-O interrogation droid]] *[[Unidentified repulsorcraft]]s *[[Jawa/Legends|Jawas]] *[[Kubaz/Legends|Kubaz]] (including [[Garindan/Legends|Garindan]]) *[[LIN demolitionmech autonomous minelayer/Legends]] *[[Maintenance droid (Mos Eisley)]] (mini-boss) *Mechanical claws *[[MSE-series|Mouse Droids]] *[[Tatooine mynock|Mynocks]]
*[[Ground Level Automated Defense Droid System]]s (GLADDS) *[[Rodian/Legends|Rodians]] (including [[Greedo/Legends|Greedo]] and [[Breedo]]) *[[Sandworm]]s *[[Saucer Droid]]s *[[Scorpion/Legends|Scorpions]] *[[Shistavanen/Legends|Shistavanens]] (including [[Lak Sivrak/Legends|Lak Sivrak]]) *[[Spidertaur]]s *[[Spitapillar]] *[[Desert stormtrooper/Legends|Sandtrooper]] or Stormtrooper officers *[[Stormtrooper/Legends|Stormtroopers]] *Stormtroopers with [[E-Web heavy repeating blaster/Legends|E-Web]] blasters *[[T0-D interrogation droid]]s *[[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE Fighters]] *[[Turret/Legends|Turrets]] *[[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]] {{C|as "Sand People"}} *[[Womp rat/Legends|Womp rats]] *[[Worrt/Legends|Worrts]] {{C|as "Drogs"}}
